assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable rather than a forecast transaction.

The receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation because the group entities have different functional currencies.
The group would satisfy the formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness requirements in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a recognised monetary item whose foreign currency risk can be considered under the fair value hedge model in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also includes a cash flow hedge model for foreign currency risk associated with recognised assets or liabilities when the exposure is viewed as variability in cash flows affecting profit or loss.
